title: 12/19/2025 — three jobs no failures, Rankett-is-it, make something from nothing
html:
12/19/2025 — three jobs no failures, Rankett-is-it, make something from nothing

Source: 2025-12-20 Google Doc. Friday-night reflection at the end of a hard week — three jobs, early shift tomorrow, then full investment in Rankett. Written ~3 weeks after the Nov financial brink doc.

TL;DR

  • The week: three different jobs worked, didn't fail a single time. "I knew this week was going to be hard, so I looked at it in the eyes and smiled, let's go."
  • Tomorrow: early-morning shift at LiftATX (Austin gym, the bridge-income overnight role). Then "finally be able to invest all of my energy into Rankett."
  • The all-in declaration: "Rankett is it. I have no other option but truly making it work. Same with Elijah. Like this is the thing that I am putting all my eggs within and I need to make this work."
  • The diagnosis: "We are close. I really just need more fire power and time. I can flow, I know I can."
  • The gratitude pivot: "God thank you. Simply for putting this in motion. I breathe in deep taking it all in. Appreciating this journey."
  • The cost named: "There are times where this feels like death. Where truly having no one around me is a curse." — first explicit naming of founder loneliness in the late-2025 set.
  • The closing manifesto: "What a blessing. I get to make something out of fucking NOTHING. Like this isn't supposed to happen and there's a chance it won't. But goddamnit I will find a way... I always do we always do."

Pattern observed

This is the Rankett-as-singular-bet doc — Yahir explicitly drops every other side-quest framing and names Rankett as the all-eggs basket. Ties Elijah Leach to the bet (explicit co-founder framing), confirms the partnership Yahir built with him is now load-bearing.

The three-jobs-no-failures bookkeeping is bridge-income hygiene — same accounting impulse as the Nov rent math doc, but in success register rather than panic register. Same week, opposite emotional valence.

The "make something out of fucking NOTHING" line is the cleanest distillation of the no runway thesis — Yahir has internalized that the obstacle (no money, no team, no help) is the proof-of-concept. Compare to the manifesto pieces that recur across this period.

The "truly having no one around me is a curse" admission is rare — Yahir mostly stays in solo-grind register. Pairs with the Austin arrival's "soft death" language: clean slate has costs.

Key quotes

  • "Rankett is it. I have no other option but truly making it work."
  • "There are times where this feels like death. Where truly having no one around me is a curse."
  • "I get to make something out of fucking NOTHING... I always do we always do."

Open questions

  • LiftATX — Austin gym, Yahir's overnight bridge income role. Worth stubbing if it recurs.
  • The "three different jobs this week" — which three? (NaviWell + LiftATX + ?)
  • "We always do" — who is "we" here? (Yahir's plural-self register, OR Yahir + Elijah?)
